title: Welcome to The Budget Brief
body: This is your personal finance feed — tips, challenges, and product updates delivered right inside your budget tracker. New posts appear automatically. No email needed.

title: The 24-Hour Rule for Impulse Purchases
body: Before buying anything over $25 in your 'Wants' category, wait 24 hours. Studies show 70% of impulse purchases are regretted within a week. This single habit can save you $200-400/month. Try it this weekend — you might surprise yourself.

title: New: Annual Analysis Spreadsheet
body: See your full-year spending trends, tax-ready reports, net worth tracking, and month-over-month comparisons. Import your app data with one click — your daily tracking feeds directly into the annual view.

title: 7-Day No-Dining-Out Challenge
body: The average person spends $300+/month eating out. Challenge yourself: cook every meal for 7 days. Track your grocery spending in the app and compare it to last month's dining category. Most people save $80-120 in just one week.

title: The 50/30/20 Rule — Your First Month
body: Don't stress about hitting exactly 50/30/20 in your first month. The real power is in AWARENESS — just seeing where your money goes changes your behavior. Most users naturally improve their ratios by month 2 without even trying.

title: Coming Soon: Debt Payoff Calculator
body: Snowball or avalanche? Our new calculator will show you exactly when you'll be debt-free and how much interest you'll save with each method. Sign up for our newsletter to be first to know when it launches.

title: Subscription Audit: Save $50+/Month
body: Go to your 'Subscriptions' sub-category right now. List every subscription you pay for. Cancel anything you haven't used in 30 days. The average person finds 2-3 forgotten subscriptions worth $30-80/month.

title: Mid-Year Financial Check-In
body: We're halfway through 2026. Export your data (Settings → Export) and look at your 6-month totals. How do your actual ratios compare to 50/30/20? What's your biggest spending category? Use this data to set goals for the second half of the year.
